product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update: Managed Microsoft AD is available in the  africa-south1  (Johannesburg) region. For more information, see  Deploy domain controllers in additional regions .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update: Managed Microsoft AD is available in the  europe-west10  (Berlin) region. For more information, see  Deploy domain controllers in additional regions .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update: Managed Microsoft AD is available in the  me-central2  (Dammam) region. For more information, see  Deploy domain controllers in additional regions .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update: Support for  workforce identity federation  in Managed Microsoft AD is  generally available . For more information, see  Workforce identity federation: supported products and limitations .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update:
Support for  joining a Windows Compute Engine VM automatically  to a Managed Microsoft AD domain is  generally available  with the following updates: 
 
 Added a new  managed-ad-force  flag to reuse an existing computer account. 
 Improved the existing  managed-ad-ou-name  flag to specify the path of the custom OU. 
 
 For more information, see  Metadata . 
 Using the automated domain join feature, you can also  join GKE Windows Server nodes automatically  to a Managed Microsoft AD domain.

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update:
Managed Microsoft AD is available in the following regions: 
 
 europe-west12 (Turin) 
 me-central1 (Doha) 
 
 For more information, see  Deploy domain controllers in additional regions .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update: Managed Microsoft AD supports applying Microsoft security baselines on your Managed Microsoft AD VMs. For more information, see  Microsoft security baselines .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update: Managed Microsoft AD has added a set of new event IDs to audit logs that you can export for a domain. For the updated list of event IDs, see  Exported event IDs .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update: The ability to  join a Windows VM automatically to a Managed Microsoft AD domain  is available in  Preview .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update: Support for the  migration of users from an existing domain  to Managed Microsoft AD is available in  Preview . Learn how to  enable permissions for migrating an on-premises domain with SID History .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update: Support for  schema extensions  in Managed Microsoft AD is  generally available . Learn how to  extend the schema .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update:
Managed Microsoft AD is available in the following regions: 
 
 europe-southwest1 (Madrid) 
 europe-west8 (Milan) 
 europe-west9 (Paris) 
 me-west1 (Tel Aviv) 
 us-east5 (Columbus) 
 us-south1 (Dallas) 
 
 For more information, see  Adding and removing regions .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update: Managed Microsoft AD protects your Managed Microsoft AD instances against virus and malwares using the operating system's built-in antivirus. For more information, see  Security monitoring and protection .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update: You can use the Google Cloud console to  enable LDAPS  and  configure multi-project access  (domain peering) in Managed Microsoft AD.

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update: Support for  schema extensions  in Managed Microsoft AD is available for  Preview . Learn how to  extend the schema .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update: Support for  backing up and restoring Managed Microsoft AD domains  is  generally available .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update:
Managed Microsoft AD is available in the following regions:

 europe-central2  (Warsaw) 
 northamerica-northeast2  (Toronto)

update: Generally available : Managed Microsoft AD now supports the use of  tags on domains .

update: Support for  audit logging  is now  generally available .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update: Support for  integrating Managed Microsoft AD with Cloud SQL  is now  generally available .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update: Support for  LDAPS  is now  generally available .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update: Support for  Fine-Grained Password Policies (FGPP)  is now  generally available .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update: Managed Microsoft AD now supports  audit logging . This feature is in the  Preview stage .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update: You can now create Managed Microsoft Active Directory instances using  Terraform .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update: The  Managed Microsoft AD SLA  has been published.

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update:
GA pricing now in effect 
 New pricing for Managed Microsoft AD is now in effect. Learn more about standard  Managed AD pricing  and view  pricing examples .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update:
VPC Service Controls  integration is now in  beta .  
 Learn more about  configuring VPC Service Controls  for Managed Microsoft AD to provide additional security.

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update:
Managed Service for Microsoft AD General Availability 
 
 Added support for deploying domain controllers from the following regions: 
 
 asia-east2 
 asia-northeast1 
 asia-northeast2 
 asia-south1 
 australia-southeast1 
 europe-west2 
 europe-west3 
 europe-west6 
 northamerica-northeast1 
 southamerica-east1 
 
 Learn about the  full list of supported regions . 
 AD-dependent VMs and apps can use multi-regional VPCs to reach domain controllers in any region, regardless of their relative locations. 
 Domain controllers from the same AD domain can be deployed in up to 4 regions, at domain creation or later. 
 The  Domain Functional Level  (DFL) of all new Managed AD domains is Windows Server 2012 R2. Domains created during the Managed AD Beta may have an earlier DFL. Learn  how to check your DFL .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update:
Trust status is stale 
 When the trust between a Managed Microsoft AD domain and an on-premises Active Directory domain is broken, the status in the Cloud Console may not be correctly updated. 
 To validate the status of a trust,  go to the Managed Microsoft AD page  in the Cloud Console and then select the domain. On the  Domain  page, in the  Trust relationships  table, select  Validate Trust . Learn more about  getting domain info .

product: Managed Service for Microsoft Active Directory
update: Managed Microsoft AD is now in beta!
